data.title: Silver Holds Steady Ahead of Key US-China Trade Talks
data.description:
Silver prices steadied around $32.50 per ounce on Friday, following a volatile week as investors adopted a cautious stance ahead of high-stakes US-China trade negotiations scheduled for this weekend in Switzerland.

The upcoming talks will see Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng meet with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent , as both sides seek to ease escalating tariff tensions.

The market is closely watching for any signals of de-escalation that could impact the broader commodity landscape.

Meanwhile, US President Donald Trump announced a preliminary trade deal with the UK—the first since the imposition of sweeping tariffs last month.

Elsewhere, US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reinforced a cautious monetary policy stance, warning of mounting risks to inflation and employment while dismissing calls for a preemptive rate cut to offset the economic fallout from tariffs.

data.title: Sweco to Buy Dutch Civil Engineering Consultancy Juust
data.description:
Sweco (SWEC-B.ST, SWEC-A.ST) agreed to buy Dutch civil engineering and urban planning consultancy Juust as it looks to grow the business.

The Swedish engineering consultancy said Friday that Juust will be immediately consolidated into the company. Juust's 2024 turnover totaled 4.1 million euros.

data.title: Bpost jumps on EBIT beat, confirmed outlook despite strike impact in Q1
data.description:
** Shares of Bpost are up 10% after the struggling Belgian postal operator beat Q1 expectations and reiterated its FY adj. EBIT outlook

** Q1 adj. EBIT was EUR 41.6 million euros ($46.77 million), down more than 40% year-on-year, but still ahead of the EUR 34.2 million estimated in the company-provided consensus

** "Despite being a weak quarter, 1Q25 results came in ahead of expectations," analysts at KBC say in a note

** The result includes EUR 6 million impact on operating income level from a two-week strike in February

** KBC analysts say they are pleased to see Bpost reiterating its outlook, considering it now includes the strike impact

** The shares are on track for their best day since September 2023 if the gains hold

($1 = 0.8891 euros)

($1 = 0.8894 euros)

data.title: The DAX Index Opens 0.79% Higher
data.description:
In Frankfurt, the DAX Index is gaining 185 points or 0.79 percent on Friday.

Leading the gains are Siemens Energy (3.22%), Daimler Truck Holding AG (2.46%) and Bayer (2.35%).

Top losers are Puma (-1.71%), Zalando (-0.95%) and Henkel (-0.53%).

data.title: European Stocks Edge Cautiously Higher
data.description:
Both the STOXX 50 and the STOXX 600 rose 0.3% on Friday, extending gains from the previous session, as traders remained cautiously optimistic about the US-UK trade deal and looked ahead to US-China trade negotiations over the weekend.

Oil and gas, autos, and healthcare were the best-performing sectors, while insurance lagged behind.

Meanwhile, investors also monitored corporate earnings.

Shares of Commerzbank edged up just 0.3%, despite the lender reporting a better-than-expected net profit in Q1.

Mediobanca shares gained 1.5% following an earnings beat.

For the week, both benchmark indexes saw only modest moves, with the STOXX 50 up 0.3% and the STOXX 600 slipping 0.1%.

data.title: Iran foreign min to visit Saudi Arabia, Qatar on Saturday, spokesperson says
data.description:
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi will visit Saudi Arabia and Qatar on Saturday and will participate in the Iran-Arab World Dialogue summit in Doha, a ministry spokesperson said on Friday, ahead of possible U.S.-Iran talks on Sunday.

A fourth round of nuclear talks between Iran and the United States is likely to take place over the weekend in the capital of Oman, and U.S. President Donald Trump is set to visit Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates next week.

Trump, who withdrew the U.S. from a 2015 nuclear deal between Tehran and world powers, has threatened to bomb Iran if no agreement is reached with his administration to resolve the long-standing dispute.

data.title: Serb-Montenegrin tie-up best bidder for Podgorica-Danilovgrad expressway design
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), May 9 (SeeNews) - Montenegro's state-run motorway operator Monteput said it has selected a joint offer by Belgrade-based Louis Berger and Podgorica-based Decom Group as the best one in a tender for a conceptual design of a planned expressway that will connect the capital Podgorica to the central town of Danilovgrad.

Niksic-based firm Geostandard will act as a subcontractor, Monteput said in a statement on Thursday, without specifying if any other bids had been submitted for the job.

The agreed value of the contract is 55,200 euro ($62,400) without VAT.

Under the tender terms, the contractor is required to draw the design within four months after the contract signing. The study will determine the optimal route and all technical parameters for the project.

Via Danilovgrad, the planned expressway will also connect Podgorica to Niksic, Montenegro's second largest city. The existing Podgorica-Danilovgrad road cannot serve as a high-speed road because it has many intersections and a large number of junctions on both sides requiring speed limits at many points, Monteput has said.

Danilovgrad's current urban and spatial planning envisages two different routes for the planned expressway - one of 11 km and another one of 18 km, depending on where the expressway will connect to the future Bar-Boljare motorway that will link the country's Adriatic coast with the northern border with Serbia.

($ = 0.885 euro)

data.title: Bavarian Nordic tops STOXX after Q1 revenue beat
data.description:
** Shares in Bavarian Nordic rise around 12% to their highest since end February after the Danish biotech's Q1 revenue came in above market expectations

** Its Q1 revenue was DKK 1,347 million ($203.08 million) against the company-compiled consensus estimate of DKK 995 million

** Kempen says the revenue was boosted by few larger existing public preparedness orders recorded in the first quarter

** If gains hold, the stock will see its biggest one-day rise since August 2024; it is at the top of pan-European STOXX 600 index

($1 = 6.6327 Danish crowns)

data.title: Kakao Unlikely to Get Meaningful Valuation Boost From Ads Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Kakao is unlikely to get a meaningful valuation boost from its projected advertising-revenue increase later this year, HSBC analyst Junhyun Kim writes in a note. The ads growth for the South Korean mobile internet company may come in at 9% on year in 2H, following an estimated 4% increase in 1H, Kim says. Though the company's introduction of feed-type ads and video ads may spur stronger growth, he notes, it is not enough to lead a significant enterprise value upgrade due to a lack of concrete monetization strategies. HSBC downgrades its rating on the stock to hold from buy and cuts its target price by 16% to KRW41,000. Shares slip 0.1% to KRW36,950. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0726 GMT - EDP made a strong start to 2025, with its first-quarter results exceeding expectations thanks a solid performance in Portugal and Spain, RBC Capital Markets' Fernando Garcia and Charlotte Mettyear say in a research note. The Portuguese utility reported a recurring Ebitda of 1.43 billion euros that topped both consensus expectations of 1.35 billion euros and RBC's estimate of 1.36 billion euros, the analysts say. The company achieved this result despite the fact that it recorded no gains from asset sales last quarter against a gain of 134 million in last year's comparable period, RBC says. The strong results benefited from high hydropower volumes that are likely to continue at least in the second quarter given that Iberian reservoirs are 20% above average as of May, the analysts say. Shares rise 4.7%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0726 GMT - Kakao is unlikely to get a meaningful valuation boost from its projected advertising-revenue increase later this year, HSBC analyst Junhyun Kim writes in a note. The ads growth for the South Korean mobile internet company may come in at 9% on year in 2H, following an estimated 4% increase in 1H, Kim says. Though the company's introduction of feed-type ads and video ads may spur stronger growth, he notes, it is not enough to lead a significant enterprise value upgrade due to a lack of concrete monetization strategies. HSBC downgrades its rating on the stock to hold from buy and cuts its target price by 16% to KRW41,000. Shares slip 0.1% to KRW36,950. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0721 GMT - Campari's results were disappointing, significantly missing consensus expectations, AlphaValue analyst Filippo Ercole Piva says in a research note. The Italian liquor maker booked first-quarter revenue of 665.6 million euros, down 4.2% organically on year, compared to the Visible Alpha consensus forecast of a 1.06% decline. The group attributed the fall to adverse calendar effects and U.S. supply-chain disruptions. The company seems to be facing difficult market conditions, particularly in the U.S., where a softer environment is emerging, the analyst says. Shares are down 3.5% at 5.84 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0717 GMT - Commerzbank's first-quarter earnings beat expectations thanks to a better-than-anticipated top-line performance, RBC Capital Markets' Anke Reingen says in a research note. Net interest income--the difference between what banks earn on loans and pay on deposits--in the first quarter was only slightly down compared with the fourth quarter, RBC says. The German bank's guidance for 2025 net interest income implies an average decline in the quarterly rate relative to the first quarter, the analyst says. Meanwhile, Commerzbank's fees picked up from the fourth quarter, driven by strong growth in its securities business, German private and small-business customers segment, and fees related to capital markets, she adds. Commerzbank shares rise 1.2%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0709 GMT - Travis Perkins's appointment of Gavin Slark, a well respected executive in the sector, removes uncertainty, RBC Capital Markets analyst Andrew Brooke says in a note. Slark is a proven operator in building materials in the U.K. and is well regarded given the value he created during 11 years at Grafton, the analyst says. "We think he is well placed to continue the work TPK has started to refocus and change the way it operates in order to better serve customers and work effectively with suppliers, as well as engage and motivate teams," the analyst adds. Shares are up 4.0% at 606.5 pence. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0658 GMT - Jaguar Land Rover said it welcomes the U.K.-U.S. trade deal as it secures greater certainty for the sector and the communities it supports. "The car industry is vital to the U.K.'s economic prosperity, sustaining 250,000 jobs," Chief Executive Adrian Mardell said in a statement. The British automaker, owned by India's Tata Motors, sells over 400,000 cars annually, with the U.S. accounting for around a quarter of global sales. President Trump and Prime Minister Starmer announced an agreement Thursday that reduces a 25% tariff on all imported cars to 10% for the first 100,000 made in the U.K. There was a blanket 2.5% tariff on U.K. cars exported to the U.S. before President Trump took office. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0656 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly higher, with IG calling the OMXS30 up 0.4% at around 2459. U.S. stock markets continued to rise on Thursday after President Trump presented a new trade agreement with the U.K., SEB analysts say in a note. "The fact that many details of the agreement are still not decided shows how pressured Trump has been to deliver a deal to calm markets," they say. In addition, Trump hinted that tariffs against China may be lowered soon and talks with China will start in Geneva over the weekend. Stock market futures are pointing upwards in both Europe and the U.S. OMXS30 closed at 2449.20, OMXN40 at 2298.23 and OBX at 1433.99. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0631 GMT - Riverstone Holdings' earnings are likely to fall in 2025, CGS International's William Tng says in a research report. As the Singapore-listed company sells its gloves in U.S. dollars, further weakening of the greenback will be a negative, the analyst says. Also, there's probably greater competition for market share among glove manufacturers this year, which could lead to margin pressure as the industry absorbs some of the foreign-exchange headwinds and raw-material price increases, the analyst says. CGS International cuts its 2025-2027 revenue forecasts for Riverstone by 8%-16%. It lowers the stock's rating to hold from add, and cuts its target price to S$0.84 from S$1.10. Shares are down 11% at S$0.765. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0609 GMT - Europe's automakers shouldn't get too excited by the tariff carve-out offered for U.K. cars, ING economists caution. Washington and London have reached a framework trade deal that would include an exemption for U.K. cars from a 25% levy imposed on the sector as part of President Trump's package of sweeping import tariffs. Still, most of the British cars sold in America are premium brands like Jaguar-Land Rover, Mini, or Rolls-Royce. That makes the deal tricky to replicate for European autos, which compete more directly with U.S. producers, ING says. "Where deals are done, the import quotas may be less generous than the one agreed with the U.K.," the Dutch bank says.(joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby))

0611 GMT - Bangkok Chain Hospital's 1Q net profit is likely to remain weak, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Nontapat Sahakitpinyo says in a research report. Factors are soft revenue growth and narrower gross profit margin from a lower mix of high-margin Kuwaiti patients as well as higher selling, general and administrative expenses, the analyst says. Also, Social Security Office-registered members at the hospital group fell slightly in 1Q from the prior quarter, driven by a reduction at the group's Kasemrad Hospital Saraburi, the analyst says. The brokerage lowers its 2025-2027 core profit estimates for the group by 7%-11%. It downgrades the stock's rating to hold from buy and lowers the target price to THB16.50 from THB18.50. Shares are 2.0% lower at THB14.90. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0550 GMT - A trade deal with the U.S. won't stymie Britain's efforts to get closer to the EU, economists at Dutch bank ING write in an investor note. Washington and London reached a framework agreement for a trade deal that would reduce American tariffs on British goods in exchange for the U.K. buying more U.S. goods. But the concessions offered by London aren't that major, ING says. And crucially, the country isn't budging on its food standards, which bar the import of many American products, meaning the deal won't jeopardise British efforts to build closer trade ties with its EU peers across the English Channel, the economists say. "Securing closer access is a key priority for the U.K. government, as it tries to rebuild the public finances," they say. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

data.title: Euro's Momentum Fades For Now, Should Pick Up Once Fed Cuts Rates, RBC Says — Market Talk
data.description: The euro's momentum has stalled for now but it should pick up when the Federal Reserve starts cutting interest rates later this year, rising to $1.17 by the end of 2025 and to $1.24 by the end of 2026, RBC Capital Markets forecasts. The euro has fallen since hitting its highest in more than three years at $1.1574 on April 21. RBC says enthusiasm for selling dollars has waned as trade tensions de-escalate. However, U.S. rate cuts should help the euro, while the currency's share of global reserves will likely increase over the coming years, it says in a note. The euro rises 0.1% to $1.1245, having hit a one-month low of $1.1198 overnight, FactSet data shows. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

data.title: EDP Makes Strong Start to Year — Market Talk
data.description: EDP made a strong start to 2025, with its first-quarter results exceeding expectations thanks a solid performance in Portugal and Spain, RBC Capital Markets' Fernando Garcia and Charlotte Mettyear say in a research note. The Portuguese utility reported a recurring Ebitda of 1.43 billion euros that topped both consensus expectations of 1.35 billion euros and RBC's estimate of 1.36 billion euros, the analysts say. The company achieved this result despite the fact that it recorded no gains from asset sales last quarter against a gain of 134 million in last year's comparable period, RBC says. The strong results benefited from high hydropower volumes that are likely to continue at least in the second quarter given that Iberian reservoirs are 20% above average as of May, the analysts say. Shares rise 4.7%. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan futures log weekly gain on US-China trade talk hopes
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures climbed for a seventh consecutive session on Friday and posted weekly gains on hopes of easing trade tensions, ahead of the U.S.-China trade talks, while higher oil prices also lent support.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) October rubber contract , closed up 0.6 yen, or 0.2%, at 301.2 yen ($2.07) per kg. The contract has risen 1.45% this week.

  • The September rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) eased 75 yuan, or 0.51%, to 14,620 yuan ($2,017.86) per metric ton.

  • The most active June butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) added 170 yuan, or 1.51%, to 11,450 yuan ($1,580.33) per metric ton.

  • U.S. President Donald Trump said on Thursday that he expects substantive negotiations with China this weekend and that the 145% tariffs on Beijing would likely come down.

  • Oil prices were up on Friday, after rising about 3% in the previous session, supported by the looming U.S.-China trade talks.

  • Natural rubber often takes direction from oil prices as it competes for market share with synthetic rubber, which is made from crude oil.

  • Meanwhile, China's exports rose faster than expected in April, while imports narrowed their declines, customs data showed.

  • However, Toyota Motor expects profit to decline by a fifth in the current financial year, weighed down by a weakness in the U.S. dollar and the impact of Trump tariffs.

  • Automobile sales could influence the intensity of vehicle manufacturing, which involves using rubber-made tyres.

  • The front-month June rubber contract on the Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform last traded at 171.4 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.5%.

($1 = 145.2900 yen)

($1 = 7.2453 Chinese yuan)

data.title: Times Universal Slapped with Winding-Up Petition
data.description:
Shen Ke, a former director of Times Universal Group , filed a winding-up petition against the company on April 30 over the alleged non-payment of bond, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Shen holds a 3% coupon bond with a principal amount of HK$10 million that fell due on Nov. 16, 2024. The property manager is currently negotiating the extension of repayment and settlement terms with Shen.

The petition is scheduled to be heard by the Hong Kong High Court on July 9.

data.title: ICU Medical Q1 Adjusted Earnings, Revenue Increase
data.description:
ICU Medical reported Q1 adjusted earnings late Thursday of $1.72 per diluted share, up from $0.96 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.28.

Revenue for the quarter ended March 31 was $604.7 million, up from $566.7 million a year earlier.

Shares of the company were up 8.4% in Thursday's after-hours activity.

data.title: REG - Celsius Resources Ld - Tranche 2 Placement Update
data.description:
RNS Number : 0550I Celsius Resources Limited 09 May 2025  

ASX/AIM RELEASE

09 MAY 2025

Tranche 2 Placement Update

Celsius Resources Limited (Celsius or CLA) (ASX, AIM:CLA) will issue an additional free-attaching option to Tranche 2 participants of the placement announced on 10 March 2025 ("Placement"), on a 1-for-2 basis, subject to shareholder approval being received ("Additional Options"). Shareholders approved the issue of the Tranche 2 Placement securities at the general meeting held on 28 April 2025 ("General Meeting") [1].

Given current market conditions and escalating geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainty, the Company, having been advised by its Placement adviser, is issuing the Additional Options as a good faith measure for investors' continued support of the Company's business and ongoing operations.

The Additional Options will be issued on the same terms as those free-attaching options approved at the General Meeting, and as the participants in Tranche 1 and Tranche 2 of the Placement are identical, all Placement participants will be entitled to these Additional Options.

The Placement options, exercisable at A$0.01 each and expiring 3 years from issue date, were approved by shareholders at the General Meeting, and are proposed to be quoted on ASX subject to satisfying ASX's conditions for admission of a new class of quoted securities ("ASX Conditions"). The Company is currently in the process of preparing a prospectus in connection with the issue of  the Placement options and, subject to the receipt of shareholder approval, the Additional Options ("Prospectus"). The Additional Options are also proposed to be quoted. In the event the ASX Conditions are not satisfied, then the Placement options and Additional Options will still be issued but as unquoted options. The Tranche 2 placement securities are expected to be issued on or around 16 May 2025.

As announced on 19 March 2025, Directors Mark van Kerkwijk and Paul Dudley participated in Tranche 2 of the Placement on the same terms as other un-related investors and are also entitled to receive the Additional Options on the same terms. Silvercorp Metals, Inc. will also be entitled to receive Additional Options in accordance with its equity participation right as announced on 15 May 2023.

A further announcement will be made in due course.

This announcement has been authorised by the Board of Directors of Celsius Resources Limited.

The information contained within this announcement is deemed by the Company to constitute inside information as stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulations (EU) No. 596/2014 as it forms part of UK Domestic Law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018.

data.title: China's April Exports Rise 8% Despite US Tariffs
data.description:
China's exports rose 8.1% in April from a year earlier, defying escalating trade tensions with the US, customs data showed on Friday.

The General Administration of Customs reported total exports of $3.157 trillion, while imports slipped 0.2% to $2.195 trillion.

The monthly trade surplus stood at $961.8 billion.

Cumulative exports for the January-April period rose 6.4% year overyear to $11.691 trillion. Imports during the same period dropped 5.2% to $8.003 trillion.

Shipments to the US fell 21% in April, while exports to Southeast Asia, the European Union, and the Middle East rose. Imports from the US were also down by double digits, customs data showed.

The trade data comes ahead of a planned meeting in Switzerland between U.S. and Chinese officials, where both sides are expected to begin talks aimed at easing tensions.

The US recently imposed 145% tariffs on a wide range of Chinese imports, prompting Beijing to retaliate with 125% tariffs on US goods.

China's central bank has taken monetary steps recently to support the broader economy, including interest rate cuts and increased liquidity injections.

data.title: Old Mutual Names Jurie Strydom as New CEO
data.description:
(07:01 GMT) *Old Mutual Appoints Jurie Strydom as CEO
(07:01 GMT) *Old Mutual: Strydom to Start Working as CEO Designate Until AGM on May 30
(07:02 GMT) *Old Mutual: Strydom to Become CEO From June 1
(07:03 GMT) *Old Mutual: Williamson to Continue as Bd Director Until Conclusion of AGM
(07:03 GMT) *Old Mutual: Williamson to Remain Available to Advise, Support CEO Until Aug 31
(07:20 GMT) Old Mutual Names Jurie Strydom as New CEO

By Michael Susin

Old Mutual named Jurie Strydom as its next chief executive officer to replace Iain Williamson, who plans to retire later this year.

The pan-African financial-services group said Friday that Strydom will start working as CEO designate alongside Williamson before taking on the role in full after the company's annual general meeting on May 30.

Strydom has been in management and board roles in the sector for decades, serving as CEO of Sanlam Life and Savings, Regent Insurance Group, Alexander Forbes Life, Old Mutual said.

Williamson will continue as board director until the conclusion of the AGM, and retire on Aug. 31, the company said.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: Citi cuts Barry Callebaut to 'neutral' citing possible savings delays
data.description:
** Citi downgrades Swiss chocolate maker Barry Callebaut to "neutral" from "buy", citing high leverage and delays in cost savings reducing profit visibility

** It says volume recovery may only be possible from H2 2026 onwards due to 12-month delay in the BC Next Level savings program

** "It is not clear in our view if Barry has passed the worst of its cash flow situations," the broker says, noting the company's CEO's statement that "consumers have not yet seen the price increase that we have just behind us" due to long supply chains

** Citi notes H1 net debt/EBITDA ratio at 6.5x and recent credit outlook downgrades make further financing challenging

** Out of 13 analysts who cover the company, eight rate it "buy" or "strong buy", four "hold", one "sell"

** Shares of Barry Callebaut slip 1.5%, underperforming the Swiss midcap index (.SMIM) which rises 0.4%
